title: write_smt2 behavior with multiple clock domains
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue
Synthesize a file with multiple clock domains into SMT-LIB, like the following:
```verilog
module top (
    input clk1,
    input clk2,
    output reg [3:0] count1,
    output reg [3:0] count2
);
always @(posedge clk1) begin
    count1 <= count1 + 1'b1;
end
always @(posedge clk2) begin
    count2 <= count2 + 1'b1;
end
endmodule
```
Example Yosys command:
```
yosys -p 'read_verilog example.v' \          
      -p 'prep -top top' \
      -p 'write_smt2 -stdt out.smt2'
```

## Actual behavior
This results in output like this:
```
; SMT-LIBv2 description generated by Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 84d6fab2, clang 11.0.3 -fPIC -Os)
; yosys-smt2-stdt
; yosys-smt2-module top
(declare-datatype |top_s| ((|top_mk|
  (|top_is| Bool)
  (|top#0| Bool) ; \clk1
  (|top#1| Bool) ; \clk2
  (|top#2| (_ BitVec 4)) ; \count1
  (|top#3| (_ BitVec 4)) ; \count2
)))
; yosys-smt2-input clk1 1
; yosys-smt2-clock clk1 posedge
(define-fun |top_n clk1| ((state |top_s|)) Bool (|top#0| state))
; yosys-smt2-input clk2 1
; yosys-smt2-clock clk2 posedge
(define-fun |top_n clk2| ((state |top_s|)) Bool (|top#1| state))
; yosys-smt2-output count1 4
; yosys-smt2-register count1 4
(define-fun |top_n count1| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (|top#2| state))
; yosys-smt2-output count2 4
; yosys-smt2-register count2 4
(define-fun |top_n count2| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (|top#3| state))
(define-fun |top#4| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (bvadd (|top#3| state) #b0001)) ; $0\count2[3:0]
(define-fun |top#5| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (bvadd (|top#2| state) #b0001)) ; $0\count1[3:0]
(define-fun |top_a| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_u| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_i| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_h| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_t| ((state |top_s|) (next_state |top_s|)) Bool (and
  (= (|top#4| state) (|top#3| next_state)) ; $procdff$5 \count2
  (= (|top#5| state) (|top#2| next_state)) ; $procdff$6 \count1
)) ; end of module top
; yosys-smt2-topmod top
; end of yosys output
```

## Expected behavior
The documentation of `write_smt2` says the following about the `<mod>_t` function:
> This function evaluates to 'true' if the states 'state' and 'next_state' form a valid state transition.

However, this isn't the case in this example, if you consider a valid state transition to be one that could occur on a clock tick in either domain. For example, a rising edge on `clk1` would transition the state to `{count1: 1, count2: 0}`, but this state would not be captured by `top_t`.

I've gathered that `write_smt2` isn't really designed to be used to reason about multiple clocks without using the `clk2fflogic` pass, but I think it'd be helpful to make this a bit more clear. At the very least I think it might be a good idea to change the docs, but it seems like it'd be ideal to have an error on trying to output a multiple clock domain design with `write_smt2`. This way a user who misses this doesn't accidentally end up with an SMT model that doesn't properly model the circuit.

I'd love to hear your thoughts, and would be happy to make a PR if you're open to a fix!
code_blocks:
; SMT-LIBv2 description generated by Yosys 0.9+2406 (git sha1 84d6fab2, clang 11.0.3 -fPIC -Os)
; yosys-smt2-stdt
; yosys-smt2-module top
(declare-datatype |top_s| ((|top_mk|
  (|top_is| Bool)
  (|top#0| Bool) ; \clk1
  (|top#1| Bool) ; \clk2
  (|top#2| (_ BitVec 4)) ; \count1
  (|top#3| (_ BitVec 4)) ; \count2
)))
; yosys-smt2-input clk1 1
; yosys-smt2-clock clk1 posedge
(define-fun |top_n clk1| ((state |top_s|)) Bool (|top#0| state))
; yosys-smt2-input clk2 1
; yosys-smt2-clock clk2 posedge
(define-fun |top_n clk2| ((state |top_s|)) Bool (|top#1| state))
; yosys-smt2-output count1 4
; yosys-smt2-register count1 4
(define-fun |top_n count1| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (|top#2| state))
; yosys-smt2-output count2 4
; yosys-smt2-register count2 4
(define-fun |top_n count2| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (|top#3| state))
(define-fun |top#4| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (bvadd (|top#3| state) #b0001)) ; $0\count2[3:0]
(define-fun |top#5| ((state |top_s|)) (_ BitVec 4) (bvadd (|top#2| state) #b0001)) ; $0\count1[3:0]
(define-fun |top_a| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_u| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_i| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_h| ((state |top_s|)) Bool true)
(define-fun |top_t| ((state |top_s|) (next_state |top_s|)) Bool (and
  (= (|top#4| state) (|top#3| next_state)) ; $procdff$5 \count2
  (= (|top#5| state) (|top#2| next_state)) ; $procdff$6 \count1
)) ; end of module top
; yosys-smt2-topmod top
; end of yosys output
